title: Coordinate Geometry
summary: Chapter 7 covers coordinate geometry — locating points on a plane using x and y coordinates. Students learn the Distance Formula to find the distance between two points, the Section Formula to find the coordinates of a point dividing a line segment in a given ratio, and the Midpoint Formula. The chapter also covers how to find the area of a triangle when its vertices are known using the coordinate formula.
learningPoints: Recall the Cartesian plane, origin, x-axis, y-axis and four quadrants
Apply the Distance Formula to find distance between two points
Use distance formula to classify triangles and quadrilaterals
Apply the Section Formula to find coordinates of a point dividing a segment internally in ratio m:n
Apply the Midpoint Formula as a special case of section formula with ratio 1:1
Find coordinates of centroid of a triangle using section formula
Calculate area of a triangle with given vertices using the coordinate formula
Check collinearity of three points using area formula
Solve problems involving distances and division of line segments
Find the point of trisection of a line segment

formulas.label: Distance from Origin
formulas.description: Distance of point (x,y) from the origin (0,0).

formulas.label: Section Formula
formulas.description: Point P dividing line joining (x₁,y₁) and (x₂,y₂) in ratio m:n internally.

formulas.label: Midpoint Formula
formulas.description: Midpoint of line segment joining (x₁,y₁) and (x₂,y₂). Special case of section formula with m=n=1.

formulas.label: Centroid of Triangle
formulas.description: Centroid divides each median in ratio 2:1 from vertex.

resources.label: Coordinate Geometry — Video Lessons
resources.description: Videos on distance formula, section formula and area of triangle.

resources.label: NCERT Mathematics Textbook — Class 10
resources.description: Official NCERT textbook Chapter 7 Coordinate Geometry.

mcqs.question: Midpoint of line segment joining (4,-2) and (-2,4) is:
The point (0,4) lies on:
mcqs.explanation: x-coordinate is 0, so it lies on the y-axis.

mcqs.question: Point dividing segment joining (1,3) and (4,9) in ratio 1:2 is:
Area of triangle with vertices (0,0), (4,0), (0,3) is:
Distance of point (5,12) from origin is:
The centroid of triangle with vertices (0,0), (6,0), (0,6) is:
If (1,2), (4,y), (x,6) are collinear and slope is same, the values satisfy:
Point P divides AB where A=(1,1) and B=(5,5) such that AP:PB = 3:1. P is:
Vertices of a parallelogram ABCD are A(1,2), B(4,y), C(x,6), D(3,5). Find x and y:
mcqs.explanation: Diagonals bisect each other. Midpoint of AC = Midpoint of BD. (1+x)/2=(1+4)/2 so x=6 — wait: (1+x)/2=(4+3)/2=3.5, x=6. (2+6)/2=(y+5)/2, y=3.

mcqs.question: What type of triangle is formed by (1,1), (1,4), (4,1)?
mcqs.explanation: Sides: AB=3, AC=3, BC=3√2. AB=AC so isosceles, and AB²+AC²=BC² so right-angled. Right isosceles.

mcqs.question: The x-axis divides segment joining (2,4) and (6,-8) in ratio:
mcqs.explanation: At x-axis y=0. Using section formula: 4m-8n=0 (wait m×(-8)+n×4/(m+n)=0). m/n = 4/8 = 1:2.

mcqs.question: Area of quadrilateral with vertices (1,0), (5,3), (7,0), (5,-3) is:
If distance between (3,a) and (8,10) is 13, then a is:

exercises.question: Find the point which divides the line segment joining (4,-3) and (8,5) in ratio 3:1 internally.
Find area of triangle with vertices (2,3), (-1,0), (2,-4).
Find the coordinates of trisection points of the segment joining (4,-1) and (-2,-3).
Determine if (1,-1), (3,3) and (-1,-4) form a right-angled triangle.
Show that the points (1,7), (4,2), (-1,-1) and (-4,4) are vertices of a square.
exercises.answer: AB=√(9+25)=√34. BC=√(25+9)=√34. CD=√(9+25)=√34. DA=√(25+9)=√34. All sides equal. Diagonal AC=√(4+64)=√68=AB√2. Since diagonals=side×√2 and all sides equal, ABCD is a square.

exercises.question: Find the ratio in which the y-axis divides the segment joining (5,-6) and (-1,-4).
exercises.answer: Let ratio = k:1. x-coordinate = (k×(-1)+1×5)/(k+1) = 0. -k+5=0. k=5. Ratio = 5:1.

exercises.question: Find the centroid of triangle whose vertices are (3,-5), (-7,4), (10,-2).
